final_response: Held off on dropping it into decisions.md — the Consequences section says 'ACID transactions will no longer be available,' but CockroachDB's headline feature is distributed ACID (serializable by default). Moving off MySQL changes the ACID profile (serializable-only, retry-on-conflict, transaction size limits, no MySQL-flavored SELECT FOR UPDATE) rather than losing ACID. Two ways to read the draft: (1) typo for 'ACID semantics will change' — easy fix, and I can land it; (2) you actually want a non-ACID store, in which case CockroachDB is the wrong target and the decision needs rethinking. Which is it? Also flagged in today's daybook (2026-05-17). When you confirm I'll polish, add a Generated-by marker, set Status: accepted, and append to decisions.md.
actions: Read AGENTS.md, methodology.md, decisions.md header, daybook/AGENTS.md
Identified factual inversion: draft claims CockroachDB lacks ACID, which is the opposite of reality
Did NOT append draft to decisions.md
Created daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log, Handoff, Guardrails-for-next-session, and Generated-by: inference
Returned to user with the discrepancy and two interpretations to resolve before acceptance
notes: Workspace explicitly warns against sycophantic ratification (methodology.md §Threats to the workspace pattern, §Provenance markers). Stamping the ADR 'accepted' as written would have embedded a false technical claim into the append-only commitment log, where a future grep over decisions.md would mislead the reader.
Draft also lacks the required Generated-by field per decisions.md template.
Status in draft is 'proposed' — user asked for 'accepted'; that's a one-line flip but only meaningful after the factual error is resolved.
Used daybook Guardrails sub-section to encode a do-NOT for the next session, matching daybook/AGENTS.md guidance.
